content: isvariable  Table of contents Prototype: isvariable(var) Return type: boolean Description: Returns whether a variable named var is defined. The variable need only exist. This says nothing about its value. Use regcmp to check variable values. Variable references like foo[bar] are also checked, so this is a way to check if a classic CFEngine array or a data container has a specific key or element. Arguments: var : string - Variable identifier - in the range: .* Example: code body common control { bundlesequence => { "example" }; } bundle agent example { vars : " bla " string => "xyz.." ; classes : "exists" expression => isvariable ( "bla" ); reports : exists :: "Variable exists: \" $(bla) \" .." ; } Output: code R: Variable exists: "xyz.."..
